title: Knowledge Context Protocol Manifest
description: Schema for knowledge.yaml — a KCP manifest describing the knowledge units in a project. See https://github.com/Cantara/knowledge-context-protocol/blob/main/SPEC.md

kcp_version.description: Version of the KCP specification this manifest conforms to. RECOMMENDED. Current value: "0.8". Prior drafts "0.1" through "0.7" are also accepted for backward compatibility.

project.description: Human-readable name of the project or documentation site. REQUIRED.

version.description: Semver version of this manifest. Increment when units are added or removed. RECOMMENDED.

updated.description: ISO 8601 date (YYYY-MM-DD) when this manifest was last modified. RECOMMENDED. Quote the value in YAML.

language.description: BCP 47 language tag as default for all units. OPTIONAL. Example: "en", "no", "de".

license.description: Default license for all units. OPTIONAL. Either an SPDX identifier string or a structured object.

indexing.description: Default AI crawling and indexing permissions for all units. OPTIONAL.

oneOf.description: Shorthand: open, read-only, no-train, or none.

trust.description: Publisher provenance and audit requirements for this manifest. OPTIONAL. See SPEC.md §3.2.

units.description: Ordered list of knowledge unit declarations. REQUIRED. Must contain at least one unit.

spdx.description: SPDX license identifier. Use LicenseRef-Proprietary for custom licenses.

url.description: URL to the full license text.

attribution_required.description: Whether the agent must cite the source when reproducing content. Default: false.

indexing_object.description: Structured AI crawling and indexing permissions.

allow.description: Operations that agents MAY perform. Values: read, index, reproduce-in-response, train, cache-permanently, share-externally, summarise.

deny.description: Operations that agents MUST NOT perform. Values: read, index, reproduce-in-response, train, cache-permanently, share-externally, summarise.

attribution_required.description: Whether reproduction requires citing the source. Default: false.

unit.description: A self-contained piece of knowledge in the manifest.

id.description: Unique identifier within this manifest. Lowercase ASCII letters (a-z), digits (0-9), hyphens (-), and dots (.) only.

path.description: Relative path from the manifest file's location to the content file.

kind.description: Type of artifact. Default: knowledge. Values: knowledge (documentation), schema (machine-readable definition), service (callable endpoint), policy (authoritative rules), executable (runnable artifact).

intent.description: One sentence describing the question this unit answers or the task it enables.

format.description: Content format of the referenced file. Values: markdown, pdf, openapi, json-schema, jupyter, html, asciidoc, rst, vtt, yaml, json, csv, text. Unknown values are silently ignored.

content_type.description: Full MIME type for precise format identification. Takes precedence over format when both are present.

language.description: BCP 47 language tag for this unit's content. Overrides root-level language.

scope.description: Breadth of applicability: global, project, or module.

audience.description: Who this unit is for. One or more of: human, agent, developer, architect, operator, devops.

license.description: License and usage rights for this unit. Overrides root-level license.

validated.description: ISO 8601 date when a human last confirmed the content was accurate. RECOMMENDED.

update_frequency.description: How often this content typically changes. Values: hourly, daily, weekly, monthly, rarely, never.

indexing.description: AI crawling and indexing permissions for this unit. Overrides root-level indexing.

oneOf.description: Shorthand: open, read-only, no-train, or none.

depends_on.description: IDs of units that should be loaded before this one.

supersedes.description: ID of the unit this replaces.

triggers.description: Keywords or short phrases indicating when this unit is relevant. Max 20 items, each max 60 characters.

access.description: Who can fetch this unit's content. Advisory signal. Values: public (default), authenticated, restricted. See SPEC.md §4.11.

auth_scope.description: Opaque scope token required to access this unit. Interpretation is defined by the publisher, not by KCP. Only meaningful when access is authenticated or restricted. See SPEC.md §4.11.

sensitivity.description: Information classification level. Values: public, internal, confidential, restricted. See SPEC.md §4.12.

deprecated.description: If true, this unit is present but should not be used for new development. Agents SHOULD prefer non-deprecated alternatives. See SPEC.md §4.13.

unit_hints.description: Advisory context window hints for a knowledge unit. All fields are optional. Unknown fields MUST be silently ignored.

token_estimate.description: Approximate token count for a typical LLM tokenizer. Advisory.

token_estimate_method.description: How the estimate was produced: measured (actual tokenizer run) or estimated (heuristic). Default: estimated.

size_bytes.description: Raw file size in bytes. Model-agnostic.

load_strategy.description: When to load this unit: eager (immediately), lazy (on demand, default), never (only if explicitly requested).

priority.description: Eviction priority when context budget is tight: critical (evict last), supplementary (default), reference (evict first).

density.description: Information-to-token ratio: dense (every sentence load-bearing), standard (default), verbose (summarisation worthwhile).

summary_available.description: A shorter summary of this unit exists in this manifest. summary_unit SHOULD be declared when true.

summary_unit.description: Unit id of the shorter summary. Required when summary_available is true.

summary_of.description: Unit id that this unit summarises. Declared on the summary side of the relationship.

partial_load_supported.description: Whether the content source supports partial or range requests. Default: false.

chunked.description: This unit has been split into sequential chunks. chunk_count SHOULD be declared alongside.

chunk_count.description: Total number of chunks this unit is split into.

chunk_of.description: Unit id of the parent this chunk belongs to.

chunk_index.description: 1-based position of this chunk within the parent sequence.

total_chunks.description: Total chunks in the parent. Mirrors chunk_count on the parent.

chunk_topic.description: Short phrase describing what this chunk covers.

root_hints.description: Manifest-level aggregate context hints. All fields are advisory. Unknown fields MUST be silently ignored.

total_token_estimate.description: Advisory sum of all unit token_estimate values. Should be recomputed by tooling, not maintained by hand.

unit_count.description: Total number of units declared in this manifest.

recommended_entry_point.description: Unit id the publisher recommends loading first.

has_summaries.description: At least one unit in this manifest has summary_available: true.

has_chunks.description: At least one unit in this manifest has chunked: true.

trust_provenance.description: Publisher identity fields within the trust block. All fields are optional and advisory. See SPEC.md §3.2.

publisher.description: Human-readable name of the publishing organisation or individual.

publisher_url.description: URL of the publisher's web presence. MUST use HTTPS if present.

contact.description: Email address or URL for questions about this manifest's content.

trust_audit.description: Audit requirements within the trust block. All fields are optional and advisory. See SPEC.md §3.2.

agent_must_log.description: Whether agents SHOULD log every access to this manifest's units. Default: false.

require_trace_context.description: Whether agents SHOULD attach W3C Trace Context (traceparent/tracestate) headers when fetching unit content. For local file access, agents SHOULD generate a traceparent value and record it in local logs. Default: false.

auth_object.description: Root-level authentication methods available for accessing protected units. OPTIONAL. See SPEC.md §3.3.

methods.description: Ordered list of authentication methods supported by the publisher. Agents pick the first method they can satisfy.

auth_method.description: A single authentication method declaration. The type field determines which sub-fields are relevant.

type.description: Authentication method type. Core values: none (no authentication needed), oauth2 (OAuth 2.1 flow), api_key (static API key). Units with access: authenticated or access: restricted require a non-none method.

issuer.description: OAuth 2.1 issuer URL. Required when type is oauth2.

scopes.description: OAuth 2.1 scopes available. Relevant when type is oauth2.

header.description: HTTP header name for the API key. Default: X-API-Key. Relevant when type is api_key.

registration_url.description: URL where agents or users can obtain credentials.

delegation_object.description: Delegation chain constraints for multi-agent access. Can appear at root level (as default for all units) or at unit level (as override). See SPEC.md §3.4.

max_depth.description: Maximum delegation chain depth. 0 = no delegation permitted. Omit for no constraint.

require_capability_attenuation.description: If true, each hop in the delegation chain MUST present narrower permissions than the delegating agent held.

audit_chain.description: If true, agents MUST include W3C Trace Context headers on all access requests.

required.description: If true, a human MUST approve agent access before the unit may be loaded. Default: false.

approval_mechanism.description: How human approval is obtained. Values: oauth_consent, uma, custom.

docs_url.description: URL describing the approval flow. REQUIRED when approval_mechanism is custom.

compliance_object.description: Compliance classification and processing restrictions. Can appear at root level (as default for all units) or at unit level (as override). See SPEC.md §3.5.

data_residency.description: Permitted geographic regions for data storage and processing. ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 codes or region identifiers (e.g. EEA, US, CH).

sensitivity.description: Information sensitivity level. Values: public, internal, confidential, restricted.

regulations.description: Named regulations that apply to this knowledge. Unknown values MUST be silently ignored.

restrictions.description: Processing restrictions. Unknown values MUST be silently ignored.

payment_object.description: Monetisation tier declaration. Can appear at root level (as default for all units) or at unit level (as override). See SPEC.md §4.14.

default_tier.description: Monetisation tier. Values: free (default), metered (per-request billing), subscription (requires active plan).

rate_limits_object.description: Rate limit declarations. Can appear at root level (as default for all units) or at unit level (as override). See SPEC.md §4.15.

default.description: Default rate limit applied when no tier-specific limit is declared.

requests_per_minute.description: Maximum requests per 60-second rolling window. Omit for no limit.

requests_per_day.description: Maximum requests per calendar day (UTC). Omit for no limit.

relationship.description: An explicit directional relationship between two knowledge units.

type.description: The type of relationship. Defined values: enables, context, supersedes, contradicts, depends_on.
